income_work.metric: Labor force participation is 69.6%, but 27% of households earn less than the $136,872 'Real Cost' self-sufficiency standard.
income_work.target: 100% of households above the Real Cost Self-Sufficiency Standard.
income_work.community_action: Support local living wage ordinances and targeted workforce development initiatives.

education.metric: 70% of adults hold post-secondary degrees, yet 21% of K-12 students are chronically absent and only 50% of 3rd graders read at grade level.
education.target: 100% of students not chronically absent; achieve 70% proficiency target.
education.community_action: Expand neighborhood-based literacy tutoring and wrap-around student attendance support systems.

health.metric: Average life expectancy is 82.9 years, but 32% of San Francisco neighborhoods fall below global health benchmarks.
health.target: 100% of population residing in healthy and safe zip codes.
health.source: SF Department of Public Health / UCLA CHIS (2024)
health.community_action: Invest in community health clinics and address environmental health disparities in vulnerable zip codes.

political_voice.metric: General election turnout is 78.9%, but falls to 55.6% for local cycles, with residents controlling <0.01% of the city budget.
political_voice.target: Maximize voter turnout across all cycles and expand democratic community budgetary control.
political_voice.community_action: Participate in local district assemblies and engage with participatory budgeting cycles.

housing.metric: 38.4% of renter households are rent-burdened, spending more than 30% of their gross income on housing costs.
housing.target: 0% rent-burdened renter households through affordable housing protection.
housing.community_action: Advocate for affordable housing expansion and community land trusts outlined in the SF Housing Element.

mobility.metric: 52% of commute trips are made by public transportation, walking, or cycling.
mobility.target: 100% safe, affordable, accessible low-carbon transportation splits.
mobility.community_action: Promote citywide expansion of protected bicycle infrastructure and transit-first street improvements.

connectivity.metric: 89.1% of households have a broadband internet subscription; 94% have active smartphone access.
connectivity.target: 100% universal digital connectivity and access for all households.
connectivity.source: ACS 5-Year Estimates / SF Digital Equity Report
connectivity.community_action: Support municipal broadband access expansions and regional digital literacy training program funding.

food.metric: 67% of low-income adults (below 200% FPL) face active food insecurity, leaving only a 43% security rate.
food.target: 100% reliable access to sufficient, healthy, culturally appropriate food for all.
food.source: SF Department of Public Health / SF Food Security Task Force (2024)
food.community_action: Incorporate food access into urban land-use strategies and defend safety-net food infrastructure budgets.

water.metric: 85% of households meet the safe and affordable baseline, leaving a 15% shortfall driven by high utility costs.
water.target: 100% of households with reliable, affordable, safe water access without utility burden.
water.community_action: Expand utility bill discount structures and protect water infrastructure affordability metrics.

energy.metric: 11.2% of San Francisco households are energy burdened, spending more than 6% of their gross income on utility bills.
energy.target: 0% energy burden through localized efficiency and affordable renewable energy access.
energy.source: US DOE LEAD Data / SFPUC Synthesis (2024)
energy.community_action: Deploy residential building electrification retrofits and community-focused solar microgrid networks.

equality.metric: Women working full-time, year-round earn approximately 87 cents for every $1 earned by men.
equality.target: Achieve full 1:1 gender wage parity and systemic economic inclusion.
equality.community_action: Support local pay transparency ordinances and equitable corporate hiring standards.

community.metric: 61% of residents report feeling respected, included, or having social systems they can rely on for support.
community.target: 100% strong, connected community spaces ensuring belonging and mutual aid.
community.source: California Wellness and City Survey Proxies (2025/2026)
community.community_action: Fund and scale community centers, neighborhood mutual aid programs, and localized network building.

peace_justice.metric: 52% of residents feel safe walking alone at night; the inverse violent crime index stands at 83%.
peace_justice.target: 100% safety, systemic justice, and deep community trust free from fear.
peace_justice.community_action: Invest heavily in non-police community safety options and neighborhood mediation infrastructure.

climate_change.metric: Consumption-based emissions stand at 4.4 metric tons CO2e per capita.
climate_change.target: Reduce consumption emissions to a safe science-based limit of 2.5 tCO2e per capita.
climate_change.community_action: Enforce commercial building decarbonization laws and accelerate public electric vehicle infrastructure.

freshwater_disruption.metric: Residential consumption is optimized at 43 Gallons Per Capita per Day (GPCD) as of FY 2024-2025.
freshwater_disruption.target: Maintain water efficiency footprints while continuously protecting total macro system withdrawals.
freshwater_disruption.community_action: Scale graywater recovery installations and promote advanced industrial loop reuse methods.

water_quality.metric: 55% of evaluated water bodies are listed as impaired on the regional integrated water quality grid.
water_quality.target: 0% impaired water body segments to maintain structural water cycle health.
water_quality.source: California State Water Resources Control Board Integrated Report (2024)
water_quality.community_action: Upgrade urban stormwater captures to minimize toxic urban runoff into the Bay-Delta ecosystem.

air_pollution.metric: Annual mean PM2.5 exposure is 7.2 µg/m³, which represents 144% of optimal WHO health guidelines.
air_pollution.target: Reduce annual mean PM2.5 levels down to the safe 5 µg/m³ boundary limit.
air_pollution.source: BAAQMD / World Health Organization Air Quality Database
air_pollution.community_action: Establish zero-emission commercial transit corridors and expand street-level urban canopy filters.

nutrient_pollution.metric: 66% to 68% nutrient overload, registering 17.3 tons/day vs the safe bay target boundary of 10.4 tons/day.
nutrient_pollution.target: Reduce marine nutrient loading to 0% overshoot above the Bayside aggregate ceiling.
nutrient_pollution.source: Bay Area Clean Water Agencies (BACWA) / SFPUC Summary Data
nutrient_pollution.community_action: Fund advanced tertiary nutrient-removal retrofits at city wastewater treatment complexes.

land_conversion.metric: 60% citywide impermeable surface coverage restricts urban soil and biological functionality.
land_conversion.target: Reduce impermeable surface coverage to a maximum ceiling threshold of 40%.
land_conversion.source: SF Environment Department / Urban Forest Buffers Mapping
land_conversion.community_action: Replace concrete yards with permeable green infrastructure and local bioswales.

biodiversity_loss.metric: 50% of vital bio-indicator species (birds and fish) inside the Estuary show ongoing biological decline.
biodiversity_loss.target: 0% indicator species in decline; ensure balanced ecosystem recovery conditions.
biodiversity_loss.source: The State of Our Estuary Report (2025)
biodiversity_loss.community_action: Support local shoreline marsh restoration projects and plant native flora corridors across city park systems.

chemical_pollution.metric: Localized consumer chemical footprints and multi-capital legacy persistent contaminants require structured baseline monitoring.
chemical_pollution.target: 0% chemical contamination over environmental absorption capacity.
chemical_pollution.community_action: Restrict municipal toxic pesticide use and mandate green chemistry purchasing programs.
